toolSpec.name: webapp_deployment_help
toolSpec.description: Get help information about using the deploy_webapp_tool to perform web application deployments.

deployment_type.description: Type of deployment to get help information for

toolSpec.name: deploy_serverless_app_help
toolSpec.description: Provides instructions on how to deploy a serverless application to AWS Lambda.

toolSpec.name: get_iac_guidance
toolSpec.description: Returns guidance on selecting an infrastructure as code (IaC) platform to deploy Serverless applications to AWS.

toolSpec.name: get_lambda_event_schemas
toolSpec.description: Returns AWS Lambda event schemas for different event sources (e.g. s3, sns, apigw) and programming languages.

event_source.description: Event source (e.g., api-gw, s3, sqs, sns, kinesis, eventbridge, dynamodb)

runtime.description: Programming language for the schema references (e.g., go, nodejs, python, java)

toolSpec.name: get_lambda_guidance
toolSpec.description: Use this tool to determine if AWS Lambda is suitable platform to deploy an application.

use_case.description: Description of the use case. (e.g. scheduled tasks, event-driven application)

toolSpec.name: get_serverless_templates
toolSpec.description: Returns example SAM templates from the Serverless Land GitHub repo.

toolSpec.name: sam_build
toolSpec.description: Builds a serverless application using AWS SAM (Serverless Application Model) CLI.

project_directory.description: Absolute path to directory containing the SAM project

template_file.description: Absolute path to the template file (defaults to template.yaml)

base_dir.description:
Resolve relative paths to function's source code with respect to this folder.
             Use this option if you want to change how relative paths to source code folders are resolved.
             By default, relative paths are resolved with respect to the AWS SAM template's location.

build_dir.description:
The absolute path to a directory where the built artifacts are stored
                This directory and all of its content are removed with this option

use_container.description:
Use a Lambda-like container to build the function. Use this option if your function requires a specific
                runtime environment or dependencies that are not available on the local machine. Docker must be installed

no_use_container.description: Run build in local machine instead of Docker container.

parallel.description: Build your AWS SAM application in parallel.

container_env_vars.description:
Environment variables to pass to the build container.
                Each instance takes a key-value pair, where the key is the resource and environment variable, and the
                value is the environment variable's value.
                For example: --container-env-var Function1.GITHUB_TOKEN=TOKEN1 --container-env-var Function2.GITHUB_TOKEN=TOKEN2.

build_image.description:
The URI of the container image that you want to pull for the build. By default, AWS SAM pulls the
             container image from Amazon ECR Public. Use this option to pull the image from another location

manifest.description:
Absolute path to a custom dependency manifest file (e.g., package.json) instead of the default.
             For example: 'ParameterKey=KeyPairName, ParameterValue=MyKey ParameterKey=InstanceType, ParameterValue=t1.micro.

parameter_overrides.description:
CloudFormation parameter overrides encoded as key-value pairs.
                For example: 'ParameterKey=KeyPairName, ParameterValue=MyKey ParameterKey=InstanceType, ParameterValue=t1.micro

region.description: AWS Region to deploy to (e.g., us-east-1)

save_params.description: Save parameters to the SAM configuration file

toolSpec.name: sam_deploy
toolSpec.description: Deploys a serverless application onto AWS Cloud using AWS SAM (Serverless Application Model) CLI and CloudFormation.

application_name.description: Name of the application to be deployed

project_directory.description: Absolute path to directory containing the SAM project (defaults to current directory)

template_file.description: Absolute path to the template file (defaults to template.yaml)

s3_bucket.description: S3 bucket to deploy artifacts to. You cannot set both s3_bucket and resolve_s3 parameters

parameter_overrides.description: CloudFormation parameter overrides encoded as key-value pairs

capabilities.description: IAM capabilities required for the deployment

config_file.description: Absolute path to the SAM configuration file

config_env.description: Environment name specifying default parameter values in the configuration file

metadata.description: Metadata to include with the stack

tags.description: Tags to apply to the stack

resolve_s3.description: Automatically create an S3 bucket for deployment artifacts.  You cannot set both s3_bucket and resolve_s3 parameters

toolSpec.name: sam_init
toolSpec.description: Initializes a serverless application using AWS SAM (Serverless Application Model) CLI.

project_name.description: Name of the SAM project to create

runtime.description:
Runtime environment for the Lambda function.
                             This option applies only when the package type is Zip.

project_directory.description: Absolute path to directory where the SAM application will be initialized

dependency_manager.description: Dependency manager for the Lambda function (e.g. npm, pip)

package_type.description: Package type for the Lambda function. Zip creates a .zip file archive, and Image creates a container image.

application_template.description:
Template for the SAM application, e.g., hello-world, quick-start, etc.
             This parameter is required if location is not specified.

application_insights.description:
Activate Amazon CloudWatch Application Insights monitoring.
                Helps you monitor the AWS resources in your applications to help identify potential issues.
                It can analyze AWS resource data for signs of problems and build automated CloudWatch dashboards to visualize them.

no_application_insights.description: Deactivate Amazon CloudWatch Application Insights monitoring

base_image.description:
Base image for the application when package type is Image.
                The AWS base images are preloaded with a language runtime, a runtime interface client to manage the
                interaction between Lambda and your function code, and a runtime interface emulator for local testing.

config_env.description: Environment name specifying default parameter values in the configuration file

config_file.description: Absolute path to configuration file containing default parameter values

extra_content.description: Override custom parameters in the template's cookiecutter.json

location.description:
Template or application location (Git, HTTP/HTTPS, zip file path).
                This GitHub repo https://github.com/aws/aws-sam-cli-app-templates contains a collection of templates.
                This parameter is required if app_template is not specified.

save_params.description: Save parameters to the SAM configuration file

tracing.description:
Activate AWS X-Ray tracing for Lambda functions. X-ray collects data about requests
            that your application serves and provides tools that you can use to view, filter, and gain insights into that data to identify issues
            and opportunities for optimization.

no_tracing.description: Deactivate AWS X-Ray tracing for Lambda functions

toolSpec.name: sam_local_invoke
toolSpec.description: Locally invokes a Lambda function using AWS SAM CLI.

project_directory.description: Absolute path to directory containing the SAM project

resource_name.description: Name of the Lambda function to invoke locally

template_file.description: Absolute path to the SAM template file (defaults to template.yaml)

event_file.description: Absolute path to a JSON file containing event data

event_data.description: JSON string containing event data (alternative to event_file)

environment_variables_file.description: Absolute path to a JSON file containing environment variables to pass to the function

docker_network.description: Docker network to run the Lambda function in

container_env_vars.description: Environment variables to pass to the container

parameter.description: Override parameters from the template file

log_file.description: Absolute path to a file where the function logs will be written

layer_cache_basedir.description: Directory where the layers will be cached

toolSpec.name: sam_logs
toolSpec.description: Fetches CloudWatch logs that are generated by Lambda function and API GW resources in a SAM application.

resource_name.description:
Name of the resource to fetch logs for. This is be the logical ID of the function resource in the AWS CloudFormation/AWS SAM template.
                Multiple names can be provided by repeating the parameter again. If you don't specify this option,
                AWS SAM fetches logs for all resources in the stack that you specify. You must specify stack_name wheみ specifying resource_name.

start_time.description: Fetch logs starting from this time (format: 5mins ago, tomorrow, or YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS)

end_time.description: Fetch logs up until this time (format: 5mins ago, tomorrow, or YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS)

cw_log_group.description:
Use AWS CloudWatch to fetch logs. Includes logs from the CloudWatch Logs log groups that you specify.
                If you specify this option along with name, AWS SAM includes logs from the specified log groups in addition to logs from the named resources.

config_env.description: Environment name specifying default parameter values in the configuration file

config_file.description: Absolute path to configuration file containing default parameter values

save_params.description: Save parameters to the SAM configuration file

toolSpec.name: list_registries
toolSpec.description: Lists the registries in your account.

registry_name_prefix.description: Specifying this limits the results to only those registry names that start with the specified prefix. For EventBridge events, use aws.events registry directly instead of searching.

scope.description:
Can be set to Local or AWS to limit responses to your custom registries, or the ones provided by AWS.
            LOCAL: The registry is created in your account.
            AWS: The registry is created by AWS.

            For EventBridge events, use aws.events registry which is an AWS-managed registry containing all AWS service event schemas.

limit.description: Maximum number of results to return. If you specify 0, the operation returns up to 10 results.

next_token.description: Next token returned by the previous operation.

toolSpec.name: search_schema
toolSpec.description: Search for schemas in a registry using keywords.

keywords.description: Keywords to search for. Prefix service names with "aws." for better results (e.g., "aws.s3" for S3 events, "aws.ec2" for EC2 events).

registry_name.description: For AWS service events, use "aws.events" to search the EventBridge schema registry.

limit.description: Maximum number of results to return. If you specify 0, the operation returns up to 10 results.

next_token.description: Next token returned by the previous operation.

toolSpec.name: describe_schema
toolSpec.description: Retrieve the schema definition for the specified schema version.

registry_name.description: For AWS service events, use "aws.events" to access the EventBridge schema registry.

schema_name.description: The name of the schema to retrieve (e.g., "aws.s3@ObjectCreated" for S3 events).

schema_version.description: Version number of the schema. For AWS service events, use latest version (default) to ensure up-to-date event handling.

toolSpec.name: get_metrics
toolSpec.description: Retrieves CloudWatch metrics from a deployed web application.

start_time.description: Start time for metrics (ISO 8601 format). Example: 2025-05-30T20:00:00Z

end_time.description: End time for metrics (ISO 8601 format). Example: 2025-05-30T21:00:00Z

function_name.description:
Lambda function to get metrics for. Set this
                        parameter if resources parameter contains 'lambda' and the function name is not same as the project_name. Typically, SAM appends a random id suffix to function names.
                        Find the name from CFN stack output. If function_name is not specified, project_name is used as function name.

distribution_id.description:
CloudFront distribution ID to get metrics for. Find the id from the CFN stack output.
                distribution_id required if the resources parameter list contains cloudfront.

toolSpec.name: configure_domain
toolSpec.description: Configures a custom domain for a deployed web application on AWS Serverless.

domain_name.description:
Custom domain name to use for the CloudFront distribution . You must already own the domain name
            and have a Route 53 hosted zone in your account. This tool does not register domain names.

create_certificate.description: Whether to create a ACM certificate

create_route53_record.description:
Whether to create a Route 53 record. When set to True, this tool creates a DNS A record
                that points to the CloudFront distribution associated with this project

toolSpec.name: deploy_webapp
toolSpec.description: Deploy web applications to AWS Serverless, including Lambda as compute, DynamoDB as databases, API GW, ACM Certificates, and Route 53 DNS records.

project_root.description: Absolute path to the project root directory

region.description: AWS Region to deploy to (e.g., us-east-1)

toolSpec.name: update_webapp_frontend
toolSpec.description: Update the frontend assets of a deployed web application.

built_assets_path.description: Absolute path to pre-built frontend assets

invalidate_cache.description: Whether to invalidate the CloudFront cache

toolSpec.name: esm_guidance
toolSpec.description: Create and configure AWS infrastructure for streaming data processing. Handles Kafka clusters (MSK), Kinesis streams, DynamoDB streams, SQS queues with Lambda functions. Sets up VPCs, security groups, IAM roles, and Event Source Mappings. Generates complete SAM templates for deployment.

event_source.description: Type of event source for which to get guidance

guidance_type.description: Type of guidance: "setup" for initial configuration, "networking" for VPC/connectivity, "troubleshooting" for issues

toolSpec.name: esm_kafka_troubleshoot
toolSpec.description: Troubleshoot Kafka streaming issues and connectivity problems. Diagnoses MSK cluster connectivity, Lambda function timeouts, authentication failures, and network configuration issues. Provides step-by-step resolution guidance for Kafka and Lambda integration problems.

kafka_type.description: Type of Kafka cluster: "msk" for Amazon MSK, "self-managed" for self-managed Apache Kafka, "auto-detect" to determine automatically

issue_type.description: Type of troubleshooting: "diagnosis" for identifying issues, or specific issue type for resolution steps

toolSpec.name: esm_optimize
toolSpec.description: Optimize streaming data processing performance and costs. Analyzes Lambda function configurations for Kafka, Kinesis, DynamoDB, and SQS event sources. Provides recommendations for batch sizes, concurrency, throughput, and cost optimization. Validates configurations and generates deployment templates.

action.description: Optimization action: "analyze" for tradeoff analysis, "validate" for config validation, "generate_template" for SAM template generation

optimization_targets.description: Optimization goals for analysis (required for analyze action)

event_source.description: Event source type for validation (required for validate action)

configs.description: ESM configuration to validate (required for validate action)

esm_uuid.description: ESM UUID for template generation (required for generate_template action)

optimized_configs.description: Optimized configuration for template generation (required for generate_template action)

toolSpec.name: secure_esm_msk_policy
toolSpec.description: Generate security-approved IAM policy for MSK Kafka ESM with scoped permissions. Uses pre-approved templates, not LLM generation.

function_name.description: Lambda function name that will process Kafka events

toolSpec.name: secure_esm_sqs_policy
toolSpec.description: Generate security-approved IAM policy for SQS ESM with scoped permissions. Uses pre-approved templates, not LLM generation.

function_name.description: Lambda function name that will process SQS messages

toolSpec.name: secure_esm_kinesis_policy
toolSpec.description: Generate security-approved IAM policy for Kinesis ESM with scoped permissions. Uses pre-approved templates, not LLM generation.

function_name.description: Lambda function name that will process Kinesis records

toolSpec.name: secure_esm_dynamodb_policy
toolSpec.description: Generate security-approved IAM policy for DynamoDB Streams ESM with scoped permissions. Uses pre-approved templates, not LLM generation.

function_name.description: Lambda function name that will process DynamoDB stream records
